rulings.Case Title: VEDDER PRICE P.C. VS. TERESA WONG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Enter Judgment By Confession
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Thursday, August 8, 2024, Line 1. PLAINTIFF VEDDER PRICE P.C.'s Motion To Enter Judgment By Confession. Petitioner's unopposed "motion to enter judgment by confession" is granted. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: GIANFRANCO MURARI VS. ELVIA ALEMAN-MARTINEZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Terminating Sanctions Or In The Alternative Further Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Thursday, August 8, 2024, Line 3. 1 - PLAINTIFF GIANFRANCO MURARI , TRUSTEE OF THE FREDERICKS-MURARI FAMILY TRUST UNDER DECLARATION OF TRUST DATED MARCH 19, 2012 AND EXECUTOR AND PERSONAL REPRESENTATIVE OF THE ESTATE OF ROBERT FREDERICKS, DECEASED Motion For Terminating Sanctions Or In The Alternative Further Sanctions. The report and recommendation of the Judge Pro Tem is granted with the following clarifications: (1) the court grants terminating sanctions and defaults are entered against the noticed defendants; (2) plaintiff may seek a default judgment after a prove-up hearing in department 505 per San Francisco Local Rule 6.6(D)(7). That department conducts prove-up hearings on Wednesdays and Fridays at 9:00 a.m. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and attaches a copy of the report and recommendation and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: LUZ RESTPERO VS. KIA AMERICA, INC., ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Thursday, Aug-08-2024, Line 2, PLAINTIFF LUZ RESTPERO'S, AN INDIVIDUAL, Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two. Pro Tem Judge Jeff Wohl, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is denied without prejudice. The tentative has been e-mailed to the parties. For the 9:00 a.m. Discovery calendar, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link (DISCOVERY, DEPARTMENT 525 DAILY AT 9:00 A.M.), or dial the corresponding number and use the meeting ID, and password for Discovery Department 525. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jeffwohl@paulhastings.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(525/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: RAFAEL GARCIA-GUERRA ET AL VS. ANTOLINI LUIGI & C. S.P.A. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Thursday, August 8, 2024, Line 15. 2 - DEFENDANT COSTCO WHOLESALE CORPORATION's MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT. Defendant Costco Wholesale Corporation's motion to strike portions of plaintiff Rafael Garcia-Guerra's first amended complaint (FAC) is denied. Garcia adequately pleads a fraudulent concealment claim to support his punitive damages request. (See Nissan Motor Acceptance Cases (2021) 63 Cal.App.5th 793, 829 ["Of course, fraudulent concealment is an intentional tort that may support a punitive damage award"].) For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Thursday, August 8, 2024, Line 11. 2 - DEFENDANT MIGUEL YNCLINO , AN INDIVIDUAL's Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint. Miguel Ynclino's motion for leave to file cross-complaint is granted. California law liberally permits cross-complaints. (CCP 428.50(c).) If cross-defendant believes Ynclino's cross-complaint is infirm, he may move against it. No trial date has been set. rulings.Case Title: BIANCA VISCUSI VS. FIREFLY HEALTH CORP, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Thursday, August 8, 2024, Line 13. DEFENDANT FIREFLY HEALTH CORP, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION DOING BUSINESS AS VELVET CANNABIS, FARID HARRISON's DEMURRER to COMPLAINT. Defendant's demurrer to the complaint is overruled in part and sustained in part. First, whether Firefly Health Corporation is a proper party is a factual dispute that can be addressed by discovery. Demurrer overruled. Second, the complaint inadequately pleads intentional infliction of emotional distress. As plaintiff concedes, 1) to be "outrageous," the pled conduct must be "so extreme as to exceed all bounds of that usually tolerated in civilized societyatrocious and utterly intolerable" and (2) plaintiff's "suffering" must be "substantial" or "enduring." (Opp. 9:23-25, 11:13.) The complaint's allegations do not reach that level, and plaintiff largely relies on opinions not binding in our courts. (Id. at 10:13-22.) Demurrer sustained with leave to amend. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)
